description:
Implement the missing scope-completion path for integrated validation failures in current-branch mode.

## Why this exists

At scope completion, configured validation commands run once on the integrated current checkout after all workers are terminal and the checkout is clean. Validation can fail because multiple independently verified tickets interact badly, because a worker missed a test, or because abandoned quiescent state was resolved incorrectly. This failure path must be fix-forward and attribution-aware; it must NOT reopen already verified worker records or rewrite history.

## What to implement

When scope-completion validation fails:

1. Capture the full validation command, exit code, stdout/stderr, duration, and working directory.
2. Build an LLM-readable validation-failure evidence pack containing:
   - epic/scope description and goal;
   - validation command outputs and failing test names/files where available;
   - list of verified workers/tickets with `commitShas`, one-line commit messages, and `touchedPaths`;
   - recent beadwork coordination notes and handoff comments;
   - current `git status --porcelain` and `git log --oneline` context.
3. Ask the coordinator/LLM to cross-reference failing files/tests against ticket commit sets and handoff context.
4. For likely in-scope fixes, create fix-forward beadwork child tasks under the active epic with:
   - failing command/output excerpt;
   - suspected related ticket(s) and commit SHAs;
   - files/tests involved;
   - coordinator rationale and safety notes;
   - clear success criteria and validation command to rerun.
5. Continue the bounded loop so these new tasks can be scheduled when ready.
6. If attribution is ambiguous, unsafe to automate, or validation output is too unclear, stop with `attention` and preserve the evidence pack in worker/run diagnostics.

## Design rules

- Previously verified workers remain `verified`; do not mutate them back into remediation.
- Recovery is fix-forward: create new beadwork work, do not reset/rebase/amend/revert current-branch history.
- Do not use brittle deterministic parsing as the sole attribution mechanism. Regex extraction of obvious failing file/test names is useful evidence, but the coordinator judgment decides the fix-forward task(s).
- Do not proceed to scope-complete review while validation is failing.
- Deduplicate across repeated supervisor polls/restarts: do not create duplicate fix-forward tasks for the same validation failure signature.
- Keep detailed logs so an operator can reconstruct exactly why a follow-up task was created.

## Tests

- Validation failure creates an evidence pack with command output, verified tickets, commits, touched paths, and scope context.
- Likely-attributed validation failures create child fix-forward tasks and continue the run loop.
- Ambiguous/unsafe failures route to `attention` without creating speculative work.
- Already-verified worker records remain `verified`.
- Repeated polls/restarts do not create duplicate follow-up tasks for the same failure.
- Scope-complete review does not run until validation passes.
- Logs include command, exit code, duration, related tickets/commits, and coordinator rationale.

## Definition of Done

- Scope validation failures have a complete fix-forward path.
- New child tasks are self-contained and actionable.
- Duplicate task creation is prevented.
- Detailed diagnostics/logging are preserved.
- lint/test/typecheck pass clean.
title: Handle scope validation failures with attribution-aware fix-forward work
